title: Why learning to code in 2025 is different (and easier) than before
post:
Learning to code in 2025 is like learning to do math in the age of calculators. You still need to understand the fundamentals—but the grunt work is gone.

Here's what's changed:

AI writes the boilerplate. You write the logic.
AI catches syntax errors instantly. You focus on architecture.
AI explains code better than any documentation. You learn faster.

I've taught coding to 50+ people in the past year. Those who resisted AI tools struggled. Those who embraced them as partners progressed 3x faster.

The mental model shift: you're not learning to code. You're learning to direct code.

This doesn't make programming easier. It makes the barrier to entry nearly nonexistent and the ceiling higher than ever.

What do you think—is AI making coding easier or just different?
